macro_segments.title: Introduction and Context: The Shoel's Exemption When the Owner Works Alongside

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and daf context
Review of previous discussion on Daf 94b: Deriving the shoel's liability for theft and loss
Comparison of shoel and shomer sachar liabilities: Why shoel has higher principal liability despite never paying kofal

macro_segments.title: The Principle of Ba'alav Imo: How the Owner's Presence Exempts the Shoel

micro_segments.title: Deriving the shoel's exemption for theft and loss when ba'alav imo through gezerah shava from shomer sachar
The principle of dayo and its limits: Can the ba'alav imo exemption extend beyond explicit liability cases?
Using the vav of v'chi yishal to learn between shoel and shomer sachar in both directions

macro_segments.title: Machlokes Amoraim: Does Ba'alav Imo Exempt Peshiah When the Owner is Present?

micro_segments.title: The dispute between Rav Acha and Ravina: Whether ba'alav imo exempts negligence (peshiah)
Opinion holding peshiah is chayav even ba'alav imo because peshiah is learned from shomer chinam via kal vachomer
Opinion holding peshiah is exempt ba'alav imo because the derasha can extend two steps removed

macro_segments.title: Analysis of the Mishnah: Ba'alav Imo in Practice—Borrowing and Hiring the Owner

micro_segments.title: The mishnah's cases: borrowed with owner working, rented with owner hired, and mixed scenarios
Why the mishnah mentions only explicit Torah cases and not those derived from drashot
Attribution to Rabbi Yehuda (socher like shomer sachar) and Rabbi Meir (socher like shomer chinam)

macro_segments.title: Rav Hamnuna's Restrictive View and Rava's Rejection

micro_segments.title: Rav Hamnuna's position: ba'alav imo requires the owner present both at time of borrowing and injury
Rava's challenge from the mishnah: owner working elsewhere still exempts when present at borrowing
The reisha and seifa of the mishnah: different work locations but same underlying labor teaches necessity of presence at sheila only

macro_segments.title: Conceptual Basis for Ba'alav Imo: Three Possible Explanations

micro_segments.title: Three interpretive approaches: arbitrary gezeirat hakatuv, unified employment contract, or quasi-ownership theory
The unified employment model: once you hire the owner to work, you cannot simultaneously rent the animal
The quasi-ownership model: shoel's liability derives from partial ownership, negated when actual owner present

macro_segments.title: Further Baraitot Rejecting Rav Hamnuna: The Temporal Requirement

micro_segments.title: The second braitah: ba'alav imo applies once animal leaves owner's reshut into borrower's for even one moment if owner hired
Rava's conclusion: presence required only at time of sheila (borrowing), not at time of shevura u'meta (injury)

macro_segments.title: Abaye and Rava's Dispute Over the Source: Rabbi Yoshiya vs. Rabbi Yonatan

micro_segments.title: Abaye holds like Rabbi Yoshiya: both criteria (owner presence and time) must be explicit; neither can be inferred
Rava holds like Rabbi Yonatan: one pasuk can teach multiple criteria; presence at sheila suffices
Abaye's reading: exemption requires ba'alav imo both at sheila and shevura u'meta; either time alone makes one chayav
Rava's reading: exemption requires ba'alav imo only at sheila; presence at sheila exempts even if absent at shevura u'meta

topical_tags.term: Rabbi Yoshiya vs. Rabbi Yonatan (derasha methodology)
